1.gl: love, i.e. affection or benevolence; specially (plural) a love-feast

2.gl: breath, i.e. (by implication) spirit, abstractly or concretely (the animal sentient principle only; thus distinguished on the one hand from G4151 (πνεῦμα), which is the rational and immortal soul; and on the other from
2.kj: heart (+ -ily), life, mind, soul, + us, + you

6.gl: a calling out, i.e. (concretely) a popular meeting, especially a religious congregation (Jewish synagogue, or Christian community of members on earth or saints in heaven or both)
6.dv: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G2564 (καλέω);

6.gl: a witness (literally (judicially) or figuratively (genitive case)); by analogy, a "martyr"

9.gl: a calling out, i.e. (concretely) a popular meeting, especially a religious congregation (Jewish synagogue, or Christian community of members on earth or saints in heaven or both)
9.dv: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G2564 (καλέω);

10.gl: a calling out, i.e. (concretely) a popular meeting, especially a religious congregation (Jewish synagogue, or Christian community of members on earth or saints in heaven or both)
10.dv: from a compound of G1537 (ἐκ) and a derivative of G2564 (καλέω);

11.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
11.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

14.gl: peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
14.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
14.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);
